jokes.text: She said that I could make up whatever reason I want. You tell me what is easiest on you. Hmm, no, it's too mean. I think you're entitled, Buster. She hates you, Michael. We'll use that one.
jokes.setup: Lucille tells Michael he can make up any excuse for her absence
jokes.punchline: Buster immediately suggests 'she hates you' and they decide to use that cruel option
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - shows Buster's casual cruelty and the family's dysfunction. Well-structured escalation from polite to brutal.

jokes.text: In my case, twice. Well, two times, allegedly.
jokes.setup: Lawyer mentions mothers can't accept their children doing something vile
jokes.punchline: Buster casually admits to doing vile things twice, then tries to add 'allegedly'
jokes.explanation: Shows Buster's obliviousness and poor understanding of legal strategy

jokes.text: The good news is, you thought I said 'frolicking.'
jokes.setup: Michael mentions his mother 'frolicking' on the beach with Dusty
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually said something much worse, but we're relieved it was just 'frolicking'
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that makes audience realize they missed something worse

jokes.text: Like when she used to lock me in the bathroom for grown-up selfish needs night. Oh, did we have a good time. What? No, it ended in tears.
jokes.setup: Buster compares his current situation to childhood bathroom imprisonment
jokes.punchline: His confused positive/negative memory of obvious childhood trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Buster's confused relationship with childhood abuse

jokes.text: Lucille 2's head in a box? Spooky.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests fake evidence of Lucille 2's severed head
jokes.punchline: Buster's casual 'spooky' reaction to decapitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement - treating gruesome murder evidence as mildly spooky

jokes.text: Okay, now inside the stair car are two heads. One bald, one spiky. Spooky.
jokes.setup: Michael escalates the fake evidence to two severed heads
jokes.punchline: Buster repeats his casual 'spooky' reaction to double decapitation
jokes.explanation: Running gag escalation - same understatement for even worse scenario

jokes.text: I almost said 'the cottage.' I changed it to 'ing on the beach.' It's 'frolicking.' 'Frolicking.'
jokes.setup: Michael explains his earlier word choice confusion
jokes.punchline: Reveals his verbal gymnastics to avoid saying something worse than 'cottage'
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier wordplay joke, shows Michael's awkward verbal evasions

jokes.text: How dare they keep track of my height while not telling me?
jokes.setup: George Michael sees 'Maeby' written on a height chart
jokes.punchline: He's outraged about height tracking rather than the cottage secret
jokes.explanation: Absurd priorities - focusing on height measurement while missing the bigger issue

jokes.text: I don't want to use 'family,' 'cause they like each other, but they're a nice group of people.
jokes.setup: George Michael describes Rebel's family
jokes.punchline: Can't call them a 'family' because they actually like each other
jokes.explanation: Perfect insight into the Bluth family dysfunction through contrast

jokes.text: it doesn't turn my stomach to see her eat an ice cream cone.
jokes.setup: George Michael describes what's good about Rebel
jokes.punchline: His romantic standard is 'doesn't disgust me while eating'
jokes.explanation: Reveals George Michael's incredibly low romantic standards and awkwardness

jokes.text: Did he finish that ice cream cone after I looked away? I don't know. I threw away all the ice cream.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks about Stan finishing ice cream
jokes.punchline: Maeby's nuclear solution to throw away ALL ice cream
jokes.explanation: Absurd overreaction that shows Maeby's extreme approach to problems

jokes.text: You gotta try this new eyebrow glue. You can eat soup again.
jokes.setup: Stan recommends eyebrow glue to Lucille
jokes.punchline: Implies his eyebrows were falling into his soup
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual detail delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: If you tried the new eyebrow glue, you could look surprised, too.
jokes.setup: Lucille says she's surprised by Stan's political interest
jokes.punchline: Stan implies she needs eyebrow glue to make facial expressions
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the eyebrow gag to Lucille's frozen face

jokes.text: there's not a lot of 75-year-old women who got a body like this. You know? I don't think that's bragging.
jokes.setup: Lucille claims her body is unusually good for her age
jokes.punchline: Michael's deadpan response that it's not actually impressive
jokes.explanation: Perfect family cruelty - Michael deflating his mother's vanity

jokes.text: Say what you will about Ron Howard as a filmmaker, but the man is not a Nazi sympathizer.
jokes.setup: Michael defends against accusations about a 'skinhead'
jokes.punchline: References the show's narrator/executive producer directly
jokes.explanation: Meta joke breaking fourth wall to defend the actual show's creator

jokes.text: I've heard things about what's really going on in that Grinch movie he made. You know, that anti-Christmas thing, with the Who-ish people?
jokes.setup: Lucille continues the Ron Howard discussion
jokes.punchline: Treats How the Grinch Stole Christmas as suspicious anti-Christmas propaganda
jokes.explanation: Extends the meta joke with Lucille's paranoid interpretation of a children's movie

jokes.text: Lottie-Dottie-DA? Oh, she's a shark, Michael.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting talking to the DA
jokes.punchline: Lucille's dismissive nickname for the district attorney
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lucille's condescending attitude through childish nickname

jokes.text: The 4th of July parade is on the 2nd? Yes. They moved it because they don't want the Hispanics to get even by using up all the supplies a day early like we do with Cinco.
jokes.setup: Michael confused about parade date
jokes.punchline: Bizarre racist logic about holiday supply competition
jokes.explanation: Shows Lucille's casual racism through absurd holiday logistics

jokes.text: 'Willy Wondy?' Who's Willy Wondy? 'Will he, won't he' do magic on our float. Won't he.
jokes.setup: Gob's pronunciation creates confusion about a person named Willy Wondy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he meant 'will he, won't he' but can't pronounce it clearly
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's pretentious vocabulary exceeding his pronunciation ability

jokes.text: They, like, convert closets to, like, organize and stuff. Who would think anything different?
jokes.setup: Gob explains the closet company innocently
jokes.punchline: Asks who would think differently while clearly thinking something sexual himself
jokes.explanation: Gob's transparent attempt to seem innocent about sexual innuendo

jokes.text: So, what do we have to do to get you into a closet, huh? Wow, you guys don't mince words, huh?
jokes.setup: Salesman uses standard sales pitch about closets
jokes.punchline: Gob interprets it as a sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection where innocent sales talk sounds sexual to confused Gob

jokes.text: I've never had any complaints.
jokes.setup: Salesman asks about Gob's 'resale value'
jokes.punchline: Gob responds as if it's about sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Continues Gob's misunderstanding of innocent business terms as sexual

jokes.text: But keep in mind, we edited, like, an hour out of this.
jokes.setup: Salesman explains they'll put Gob in a specific closet setup
jokes.punchline: Breaks fourth wall to acknowledge heavy editing of embarrassing content
jokes.explanation: Meta joke acknowledging the show's editing while suggesting worse content exists

jokes.text: If they're listening, just say 'dogshit.' Mm. What you said, I would repeat, but the family is here, and I want to make sure that I don't get it wrong. Get it wrong? Say 'dogshit.'
jokes.setup: Barry gives Michael a code word for the fake phone call
jokes.punchline: Michael's elaborate euphemisms while Barry just wants him to say 'dogshit'
jokes.explanation: Absurd contrast between Michael's politeness and Barry's crude directness

jokes.text: In the 30 years that I've represented this family, that is the first time anyone has said 'good j...'
jokes.setup: Michael saying 'good job' to Barry
jokes.punchline: Barry's revelation about never receiving praise in 30 years
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that adds depth to Barry while being genuinely sad and funny. The interrupted word adds to the pathos.

jokes.text: It's just so mundane. I don't want to bore everyone. It's just so mundane. I'll get out of your hair.
jokes.setup: Lucille wants to speak privately about something
jokes.punchline: Overemphasizes how 'mundane' it is, making it obviously important
jokes.explanation: Lucille's transparent attempt to downplay something clearly significant

jokes.text: People are gonna think, 'Here comes the next Steve Gobs.' They're gonna want... Jobs. Jobs, for sure, but they're also gonna want money.
jokes.setup: George Sr. mispronounces 'Steve Jobs' as 'Steve Gobs'
jokes.punchline: George Michael corrects him, leading to confusion about employment vs. the person
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that creates momentary confusion

jokes.text: Oh, boy. There's some of that Hollywood BS. There it is. Right there. That's how they use people.
jokes.setup: George Michael mentions Rebel gave him money to support him
jokes.punchline: George Sr. immediately spins supportive gesture as exploitation
jokes.explanation: Shows George Sr.'s paranoid worldview where kindness must be manipulation

jokes.text: Look at this. Three generations of Bluths in one place. Anybody have a camera? On my phone. Yes, sir. Oh, no. It's a figure of speech. I gotta run.
jokes.setup: George Sr. suggests a three-generation photo opportunity
jokes.punchline: Immediately flees when George Michael actually has a camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of George Sr.'s fake sentiment and instant panic when held accountable

jokes.text: Uh, west. Well, I'll hang out with you at the, uh, beach. Oh, uh... No. Why did I say west? East is where I'm going. Northeast. Northeast, actually.
jokes.setup: George Sr. tries to lie about his destination
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly flustered and changes direction multiple times
jokes.explanation: Shows George Sr.'s terrible lying skills under pressure

jokes.text: You know me. I'm a 20th-century guy. You can keep that futuristic stuff. Okay? Okay. Prismo. Begin. User, Michael Bluth. Lost my keys.
jokes.setup: George Sr. claims to reject modern technology
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses voice-activated AI assistant
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocrisy reveal - contradiction happens within seconds

jokes.text: A good lie is head in a box, but they wanted to go with something real, so we went with the evidence they actually had.
jokes.setup: Barry explains why the fake evidence story wasn't better
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests decapitation as a 'good lie' while revealing real evidence exists
jokes.explanation: Barry's casual attitude toward gruesome fake evidence while accidentally revealing truth

jokes.text: A good lie is head in a box, but they wanted to go with something real, so we went with the evidence they actually had.
jokes.setup: Barry explaining the fake evidence plan to Lucille
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing they're using real evidence in their fake story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Barry moment where his incompetence accidentally reveals crucial information

jokes.text: I will not let that woman steal everything I've taken from her.
jokes.setup: Lucille expresses determination about Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Admits she's already stolen from Lucille 2 while claiming victimhood
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Lucille's twisted logic and narcissistic worldview

jokes.text: Look, I have to be careful, okay? These are not tech-savvy people. The other day, I turned the lounge TV on, and now that's my job. They call me 'Buttons' now. Buttons.
jokes.setup: Maeby explains her careful phone behavior at the senior facility
jokes.punchline: Simple act of turning on TV makes her the designated tech person with nickname
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from simple action to permanent job and nickname

jokes.text: Wow, I was just 'specu-lying.'
jokes.setup: George Michael says Maeby was right about his father and Rebel
jokes.punchline: Maeby's made-up portmanteau of 'speculating' and 'lying'
jokes.explanation: Clever neologism that perfectly captures Maeby's casual dishonesty

jokes.text: I mean, I was just day plotting. ... Wait, that's a thing? I mean, I was just day plotting.
jokes.setup: Maeby mentions plotting about estrogen
jokes.punchline: Another made-up phrase, then acts casual when told estrogen is real
jokes.explanation: Continues Maeby's pattern of making up words to avoid responsibility

jokes.text: That's a thing, too? I was just crime-storming. You know, just lying out loud. How funny.
jokes.setup: Maeby suggests Alzheimer's as a fake illness
jokes.punchline: Another made-up term followed by casual admission of malicious lying
jokes.explanation: Escalates the pattern with increasingly dark content delivered casually

jokes.text: God. Why are all the cute ones so dumb?
jokes.setup: Closet salesmen suggest a double magic act with Tony Wonder
jokes.punchline: Gob calls them dumb while completely missing their good idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Gob is the dumb one but projects it onto others

jokes.text: Like Alzheimer's? That's a thing, too? I was just crime-storming.
jokes.setup: Maeby suggesting faking a memory illness
jokes.punchline: Her surprise that Alzheimer's is real and her term 'crime-storming'
jokes.explanation: Another good neologism that parallels 'brainstorming' but for criminal activities. Shows Maeby's disconnect from reality.

jokes.text: Yeah. A classic two-fister. An old-fashioned will he, won't he?
jokes.setup: Discussing a magic trick for Gob and Tony
jokes.punchline: The sexual innuendo in 'two-fister' delivered completely innocently
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre that works because of the innocent delivery contrasted with Gob's sexual confusion context.

jokes.text: Oh, that's north. You said you were going northeast. Well, I'm headed north, then I'm headed east. Otherwise you don't pass the Andersen's P.S.
jokes.setup: George Michael catches Michael in a geographical lie
jokes.punchline: Michael's convoluted explanation about passing Andersen's Pea Soup
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's poor lying skills when caught in contradiction

jokes.text: No, thank you. Uh, nein, danke.
jokes.setup: Someone offers ice cream at the border
jokes.punchline: Michael responds in German at the Mexico border
jokes.explanation: Absurd wrong-language response showing Michael's nervousness

jokes.text: If you could tear anything in two, you'd still have a shot at me.
jokes.setup: George Sr. threatens to tear Dusty in two
jokes.punchline: Lucille's brutal emasculation about his physical weakness
jokes.explanation: Devastating insult that combines physical and sexual inadequacy

jokes.text: Lindsay? That dumb blonde slut my moron son keeps calling every five minutes since she came back on Cinco de Mayo?
jokes.setup: George Sr. and Tobias ask about Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Brutally insulting description from potential mother-in-law
jokes.explanation: Harsh but effective character establishment through insults

jokes.text: Well, if you mean is my son anything like his father, well, he's never had any complaints.
jokes.setup: Asked about his gag reflex for cleaning ostrich mess
jokes.punchline: George Sr. misunderstands as sexual question and brags inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable sexual misunderstanding in innocent context

jokes.text: Since when is Viagra pink? Your mom says that the Medicare only does the generic. You know, this is estrogen.
jokes.setup: George Sr. gets his mail including what he thinks is Viagra
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Lucille has been secretly giving him estrogen for two years
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation delivered as perfect punchline - explains George Sr.'s character changes

jokes.text: All right, well, then, I guess all that's left to say is lights, camera, action!
jokes.setup: George Michael discovers Rebel is on a movie set, not with his father
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly dramatic movie reference to cover his embarrassment
jokes.explanation: George Michael's awkward attempt to play off his suspicious behavior

jokes.text: You're probably not impotent, either.
jokes.setup: Michael's distrust of his father's explanations
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of impotence as another lie
jokes.explanation: Good callback that ties together the estrogen revelation with Michael's general distrust of his father's claims.

jokes.text: I'm okay. The bathroom doesn't work back here, so I peed in a Wetzel bag.
jokes.setup: Barry emerges after the car chase and crashes
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions peeing in a pretzel bag as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross and inappropriate Barry behavior delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: ♪ Mexican Romney Family ♪
jokes.setup: Discovery that the land belongs to Mexican Romneys
jokes.punchline: Musical theme parody treating them like a TV family
jokes.explanation: Absurd musical treatment of bizarre plot development

jokes.text: I wouldn't say I deserved an Oscar, but...
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Oscar signed over the land
jokes.punchline: George Sr. misunderstands as the Academy Award and humbly demurs
jokes.explanation: Classic name confusion with George Sr.'s vanity

jokes.text: Two guys coming out of the closet! How hard is that to understand? One is gonna be well-organized. The other one is just gonna be shredded all apart.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his magic trick concept
jokes.punchline: The metaphor of organized vs. shredded closets representing different types of gay people
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre that works both as literal closet organization and metaphor for different approaches to being gay. Shows Gob's confusion.

jokes.text: I need two closets! Two guys coming out of the closet! How hard is that to understand?
jokes.setup: Gob tries to explain his magic trick concept
jokes.punchline: Completely oblivious to the sexual implications of his phrasing
jokes.explanation: Gob's continued obliviousness to obvious double entendres
